title: Source-backed SMHC0 firewall/fabric/firmware handoff audit
question: Can local public/vendor sources or preserved artifacts identify a concrete SMHC0 master permission, SPC/TZMA, MBUS/NSI, CCU security switch, or firmware handoff bit that explains IDMAC descriptor fetch starvation?
findings: Public A733 U-Boot remains PIO/AHB evidence only; it does not exercise SMHC0 internal IDMAC descriptor fetch.
The preserved H028 firmware inventory already found no local boot0, BL31/ATF, SCP, secure-monitor, NSI/MBUS, IOMMU, or storage-master permission delta suitable for a behavior patch.
The preserved H069 register/artifact audit already found no unclosed named SDMMC0 firewall/security/master-permission register.
A fresh source/artifact sweep found no new SMHC0-named SPC/TZMA/MBUS/NSI/CCU security bit and no boot blob/source package that exposes a Linux-applicable handoff.
Package metadata still leaves BOOT0 and BL31 provenance unresolved; no package matching boot0, bl31, or trusted was found in the preserved metadata.
decision: Do not patch behavior from H085. The remaining independent path is operational: build/use a lab-only PIO root/runtime path so Cubie2/Cubie3 can keep booting far enough for userspace inspection while the real IDMAC root cause awaits maintainer/vendor reply or newly found firmware/security source.
next: Queue H086 as the lab-only PIO root/runtime proof plan. Keep it explicitly non-upstream and do not modify the public export.
